page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via 3600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81293.61 and a PassMark CPU score of 55910.68. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, registering 4502.68 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 215.93 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. Database and caching performance are notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 75.03 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring 21673.68. Conversely, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1386.79. Large-model LLM text generation is also a bottleneck, with the medium 7B model achieving only 5.7 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5dn.24xlarge offers a dense allocation of memory and high-speed local NVMe storage, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that require high-throughput local scratch space without the overhead of external storage. The combination of 100 Gbps network bandwidth and high memory capacity fits large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching layers like Redis, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications or real-time LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. It includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 81293.61 and high memory read bandwidth of 215.93 GB/sec for large blocks. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by 75.03 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance is weak, and LLM text generation speeds are poor. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and high-throughput network applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining high-capacity DDR4 RAM, fast local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps networking for demanding database workloads.

768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
